task:
# Review findings:

## Finding 1 (package.json:186-188) [added]
[P2] Deleted tests still execute from stale out/ artifacts

Deleting `src/test/components/activeSessionUi.test.ts` is not enough because `pretest` recompiles into `out/` without cleaning it first. The stale `out/test/components/activeSessionUi.test.js` still runs, which pollutes test results and can keep removed coverage failing until someone manually clears `out/`.

## Finding 2 (package.json:143-167) [added]
[P1] Drops checkpoint detail flows instead of refactoring them

The manifest now omits the explain/raw-transcript/rewind commands, and the extension only registers `openCommitChanges`. Combined with the deleted detail-panel module, this removes checkpoint inspection and rewind from the product rather than just reorganizing the UI. If that is intentional, isolate it as a breaking-change commit; otherwise keep the old commands until a replacement exists.

## Finding 3 (src/test/checkpoints/module.test.ts:213) [added]
[P1] New module assertion contradicts live-overlay semantics

`listSessionCards()` is documented to apply live `.git/entire-sessions` overlays, so this new expectation is wrong once the fixture writes a current `last_interaction_time` for `2026-03-30-alpha`. This is one of the reasons the targeted test run is red; if you want transcript-based timestamps, assert them on `listSessionsForCheckpointIds()` instead.

## Finding 4 (src/test/components/sessionsUi.test.ts:159-161) [added]
[P2] Sessions UI test is timezone-dependent

This expectation hard-codes the UTC rendering of a `Z` timestamp, but `formatShortTimestamp()` uses the local timezone. In the current environment (`Africa/Nairobi`, UTC+3), the test gets `04-04 12:00`, so this new test will fail outside UTC.

## My request for Codex:
Commit the changes
